value_proposition: Compile an existing repository knowledge graph into a concise English learning video with deterministic scenes, source grounding, strict duration, subtitles, and verifiable provenance.
target_segment: Developer teams and open-source maintainers already using or able to generate Understand-Anything graphs

target_user.description: A developer or technical collaborator learning an unfamiliar repository.
target_user.value_or_need: A short replayable mental-model primer with links back to the exact source snapshot.

economic_buyer.description: An engineering, developer-experience, or open-source lead responsible for onboarding and explanation quality.
economic_buyer.value_or_need: Reusable onboarding output that can be regenerated when source changes and independently checked for provenance.

willingness_to_pay_or_internal_roi_hypot: A team will adopt the workflow if a sub-three-minute grounded walkthrough improves the first architecture task without more than ten minutes of operator setup after graph generation.
revenue_or_economic_value_model: Potential value comes from reduced repeated explanation effort and faster repository orientation; no saved-time or willingness-to-pay claim is made before user evidence exists.
acquisition_distribution_hypothesis: Developers will discover the tool through a Codex-compatible skill, a pnpm CLI, the Understand-Anything ecosystem, and a credential-free sample video run.
adoption_friction: The target repository must have a valid and sufficiently fresh Understand-Anything graph
Strict provenance may block convenient but stale or dirty runs
Production English speech may require an authorized paid provider
Teams must trust a programmatic visual style rather than a hand-edited video
key_partners_or_dependencies: A configured speech provider for final narration

rough_cost_drivers.basis: TypeScript graph, storyboard, caption, renderer, and verifier implementation plus tests
Narration characters multiplied by the configured provider price; capped per run
CPU time, temporary frames, and final 1080p H.264 output

alternatives_and_differentiation.differentiation: The proposed extension compiles the graph into a bounded, narrated, subtitled, reproducible artifact instead of replacing exploration.
The compiler binds scenes and claims to a Git snapshot and can regenerate receipts when inputs change.
Deterministic validation restricts references, scene types, duration, subtitles, and output provenance.

defensibility_hypothesis: The durable advantage would be the combination of graph-native source grounding, deterministic media compilation, and governance-bound receipts rather than a proprietary prompt.

material_risks.description: A fluent narration may make a weak or unrelated source reference look authoritative.
Untrusted repository content may attempt prompt, path, subtitle, shell, or renderer injection.
Upstream open-source capability could be misrepresented as new work or used without notice.
Measured narration and deterministic 1080p rendering may not fit the D0 deadline.
The video may be polished without improving a developer's learning outcome.
